Name: Notion
Pricing: Free / Plus $12/mo / Business $24/mo / Enterprise custom / Custom Agents $10 per 1,000 credits
Key Features: Docs + databases + wikis + projects + forms + sites + mail + calendar
AI Features: Notion AI Core + Agent + Meeting Notes + Enterprise Search + Research mode + Custom Agents beta
Weakness: Cloud-only - local files are still second-class - agent usage is increasingly credit-metered
Notes: Shifted from AI add-on pricing to an AI workspace + agents narrative

Name: Obsidian
Key Features: Local markdown vault + plugins + web clipper + CLI
AI Features: No built-in AI - AI remains plugin-led and community-driven
Weakness: No native agent layer - no built-in web collaboration - plugin sprawl
Notes: Still the closest local-first comparison, but Cabinet is more opinionated for teams + agents

Name: Confluence
Key Features: Enterprise wiki + Jira integration + whiteboards + databases
AI Features: Rovo Search + Chat + Agents with per-user credits
Weakness: Heavy Atlassian surface area - AI value depends on their ecosystem
Notes: Rovo is now core to positioning, which makes AI-native comparison content stronger

Name: Slite
Key Features: Team knowledge base + AI search + public docs
AI Features: Ask AI search and answers, Ask in Slack, Knowledge Suite with Enterprise Search
Weakness: Cloud-only - enterprise search lives in a higher bundle - no local file ownership
Notes: Pricing shifted and Premium was replaced by Knowledge Suite

Name: Coda
Notes: Good for showing Cabinet simplicity advantage

Name: Outline
Weakness: No AI agents — no embedded apps — limited file types
Notes: Open-source competitor — we have more features

Name: Slab
AI Features: AI Autofix + AI Predict + AI Ask
Weakness: No self-hosting - AI is lighter than full agent workflows - not file-based
Notes: More AI surface area than the old row reflected, but still a conventional hosted wiki

Name: GitBook
Pricing: Free / Premium $65 per site + $12/user / Ultimate $249 per site + $12/user / Enterprise / AI add-on $149/mo
Key Features: Developer docs + Git sync + API playgrounds + authenticated docs
AI Features: LLM optimizations + MCP server + GitBook Agent beta + AI Assistant
Weakness: Documentation-first - not a full company OS - no local-first ownership
Notes: Strongest docs-specific AI story right now because every published site can expose an MCP server

Name: Capacity
Notes: Not a real competitor for indie founders

Name: Tettra
Key Features: Internal knowledge base + Slack + internal Q&A
AI Features: AI answers + AI bot in Slack + page tagging + FAQ generation + thread summaries
Weakness: Slack-centric - not file-based - thinner product surface outside Q&A
Notes: AI positioning is much stronger now than the old 'basic KB' framing

Name: AppFlowy
Weakness: Early stage — limited features — small community

Name: AnyType
Weakness: No AI at all — complex UX — niche audience

Name: Logseq
Weakness: Outliner-only — no WYSIWYG — steep learning curve
